title: Creamy Brazilian Vegetable Gratin
description: A tender and romantic Brazilian dish featuring a variety of vegetables in a creamy sauce, topped with Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese. This mustardy gratin is perfect for a special occasion.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pot of boiling salted water, blanch the sliced zucchini and yellow squash for 3 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots to the pot and continue to boil for an additional 3 minutes.
Drain the vegetables and set them aside.
In a large skillet, saute the chopped onion and minced garlic in a little bit of oil until they are tender.
In a separate saucepan, combine the heavy cream, whole-grain mustard, salt, and black pepper, and bring the mixture to a simmer over medium heat.
Reduce the heat to low and let the cream sauce simmer for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
In a large bowl, combine the blanched vegetables, sauteed onion and garlic, and the cream sauce, and toss until the vegetables are well coated.
Transfer the vegetable mixture to a slate-tile vessel and top with the shredded Parmigiano-Reggiano cheese.
Bake the gratin in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the vegetables are tender.
